title: Wet-felted pebble soap dish
subtitle: A small oval felt bowl that holds a soap bar and drains between uses
excerpt: A flat oval of dense wet-felted wool, shaped into a shallow dish over a soap bar while still damp, makes a practical soap holder. Wool drains and dries between uses, keeping the bar dry and extending its life. The project is a compact introduction to shaped wet felting with no resist; the shaping happens after fulling, while the felt is malleable.
sourceNotes: Wet-felted soap dish pattern. Technique follows standard wet-felting practice. Observation-led.

glossaryTerms.definition: Wet felting in which the piece is moulded into a three-dimensional form while still damp, after fulling. The wool retains the imposed shape as it dries. No resist is used; the shaping happens by hand.

content.text: without a resist. The panel is felted flat, then shaped into a shallow dish by pressing it over a soap bar while still damp. The finished dish is about 12 cm long and 8 cm wide, with 1-cm raised sides. It holds a standard rectangular soap bar and allows drainage between uses.

attrs.title: Water temperature
attrs.body: Use 50-60°C water throughout. Test on the back of the wrist before submerging hands.

content.text: Lay out three crossed layers of roving in an oval roughly 20 x 15 cm on the bamboo mat. This is larger than the finished dish to allow for shrinkage.

Cover with the felting net. Pour hot soapy water over the oval. Rub in circular motions for 5 minutes. Lift the net and check that the oval holds together when a corner is lifted. Continue rubbing for another 5 minutes if the fibres are still separating.

Remove the net. Rub the surface firmly for 12 to 15 minutes, adding soap and hot water as needed. Roll in the bamboo mat and press along the roll. Unfurl, rotate 90 degrees, re-roll, and press again. Repeat three times. The panel should feel firm and dense when pressed between the fingers: a soap dish needs to be fully dense so it does not absorb excess water.
Step 4: Shape over the soap bar

While the panel is still damp, place the soap bar in the centre. Gently press the panel up around the sides of the bar and hold for one minute. Remove the bar. Place the dish upside down on a folded towel and allow to dry in the imposed shape.

attrs.heading: Common problems
attrs.intro: One common problem with this project.

items.symptom: Dish flattens out as it dries and loses the sides
items.cause: Felt was not dense enough when shaped, or the sides were not held in position long enough.
items.fix: Stuff the damp dish with a crumpled cloth and allow to dry fully before removing the stuffing. The felt holds the shape once completely dry.
